_doc: Before/after LLM-judge fixtures across 4 genres (tweet/linkedin/readme/memo). Each fixture: an AI-shaped `before`, a good human `after`, and what we expect. `before_band_min`/`before_band_max`/`after_band_max` are detector score-band expectations (mechanical, deterministic regression guards bracketing the observed score). `rubric_focus` lists which rubric dimensions the LLM judge should weigh. Difficulty is calibrated so a NAIVE baseline (flag-if-detector-score >= baseline_threshold) misses ~30-40% of the DETECTOR-TARGETED `before` items: the `subtle` ones are AI-written but score LOW because they avoid vocabulary tells and lean on structural/rhythm tells a flat threshold underweights. See README calibration section. `expected_verdict` (PASS|FAIL) is the owner's gold label for advisory judge-vs-gold agreement: all current fixtures are good rewrites (PASS); FAIL exemplars live in the unit tests (test_fixtures.py), not here, to preserve the 30-40% calibration band. `detector_blind: true` marks JUDGE-ONLY fixtures (the over-stepping / 反代入式越位感 dimension): the tell is in STANCE, not vocabulary, so the mechanical detector is blind to it by design (scores ~0). Such a `before` is a MISS by construction, not a calibration failure, so detector_blind fixtures are EXCLUDED from the naive-baseline miss-rate denominator (the 30-40% band still measures only the detector-targeted set); their score BANDS are still checked. Every detector_blind fixture also carries expect_baseline='miss'. Do NOT 'fix' the calibration band by deleting that exclusion filter.

fixtures.before: In today's ever-evolving landscape, we're thrilled to delve into a game-changing paradigm. This seamless, robust solution will revolutionize how you work. The future looks bright! 🚀 #innovation #growth #tech #startup #ai #future #buildinpublic
fixtures.after: Shipped the new import flow today. Cut setup from 11 steps to 3. Early users went from 40% drop-off to 12%.

fixtures.before: Spent the week thinking about retrieval. Keyword search is underrated. Vector search is overhyped. Most teams reach for embeddings when an index would do. Worth sitting with that.
fixtures.after: Spent the week on retrieval. Keyword search beat our embedding setup on the eval — cheaper, and recall was 4 points higher. We were reaching for vectors out of habit.
fixtures.subtle_tell: negative parallelism (X is underrated / Y is overhyped), vague 'worth sitting with that' closer, zero specifics — all AI structural tells that carry NO vocabulary words, so a flat detector threshold scores it low and misses it.

fixtures.before: I recently had the pleasure of attending a pivotal conference. It was truly a transformative experience that underscored the importance of fostering meaningful connections. Moreover, the keynote was a testament to the power of innovation. As we navigate this dynamic ecosystem, let's embrace the journey. What's next? Only time will tell.
fixtures.after: Went to the data conf last week. One talk stuck with me: a team at Stripe cut their feature pipeline from 6 hours to 40 minutes by dropping a caching layer nobody had questioned in two years. Sometimes the win is deleting code.

fixtures.before:
Three things I learned shipping our first ML model.

First, data quality matters more than model choice.

Second, stakeholders care about latency, not accuracy.

Third, the simplest baseline is often hard to beat.

What would you add?
fixtures.after: We shipped our first ranking model last month and I was wrong about almost everything going in. I thought the model would be the hard part. It wasn't — a week of cleaning label noise moved offline NDCG more than any architecture change. And the thing the PM actually cared about was p99 latency, which I'd barely measured.
fixtures.subtle_tell: rule-of-three list scaffolding + 'What would you add?' engagement-bait closer. Reads clean to a vocabulary detector (no tier-1 words) so a flat threshold misses it; an LLM judge keyed to STRUCTURE catches it.

fixtures.before:
## Overview

This comprehensive, cutting-edge library leverages a robust architecture to seamlessly streamline your workflow. It empowers developers to harness the full potential of modern tooling. Whether you're a beginner or an expert, this powerful solution has you covered.
fixtures.after:
## What this does

Parses CSV files up to 2GB without loading them into memory. Streams rows, so it works on machines with 512MB of RAM. No dependencies outside the standard library.

fixtures.before:
## Installation

To get started, simply install the package. Once installed, you can begin using it right away. The setup process is designed to be straightforward and intuitive, ensuring a smooth onboarding experience for users of all skill levels.
fixtures.after:
## Installation

```
pip install fastcsv
```

Python 3.9+. No other dependencies.
fixtures.subtle_tell: all filler, zero content ('simply', 'right away', 'smooth onboarding experience') but no flagged vocabulary — it's empty calories that a word-list detector rates clean. An LLM judge measuring information density catches it.

fixtures.before: It is important to note that, as we move forward, we must delve into the intricacies of our strategy. Experts believe that fostering a culture of innovation is paramount. Furthermore, this pivotal initiative will undoubtedly elevate our position. Let's break this down step by step to ensure a holistic approach.
fixtures.after: We're behind on Q3 targets by 14%. Two causes: the API migration slipped three weeks, and we lost two engineers. Proposed fix: pause the redesign, put everyone on migration, revisit the redesign in October.

fixtures.before: While our current approach has served us well, there is room for improvement. It is important to note that research suggests engagement is trending in a positive direction. That said, we should remain mindful of potential headwinds, and a measured strategy could potentially position us well. Ultimately, this balanced approach will help us capture emerging opportunities going forward.
fixtures.after: Sign-ups are up 9% since the March redesign, but week-2 retention dropped from 31% to 24%. We're acquiring people the product doesn't keep. Before we spend more on acquisition, I want a week to dig into the retention cliff.
fixtures.subtle_tell: false concession ('served us well, but...'), hedge-stacking ('could potentially position us'), vague-attribution ('research suggests') with NO numbers. This is the BORDERLINE fixture: it scores ~15, just above the naive threshold, so the mechanical detector catches it but only barely — an LLM judge is still needed to confirm the rewrite added real specifics rather than just swapping words.

fixtures.before:
## Setup

The installer writes a config file to `~/.toolrc` and adds the binary to your PATH. Can you set this up yourself? Yes — and it's simpler than you might think. Run `tool init` and follow the two prompts.
fixtures.after:
## Setup

The installer writes a config file to `~/.toolrc` and adds the binary to your PATH. You can set this up yourself; it's fairly simple. Run `tool init` and follow the two prompts.
fixtures.subtle_tell: Over-stepping (反代入式越位感): self-Q&A theater ('Can you...? Yes —') + 'simpler than you might think' presumes the reader's doubt and answers it for them. The surrounding setup sentences are identical in before/after; only the over-stepping sentence differs. Zero vocabulary tells -> detector scores ~4 -> a flat threshold misses it; only a judge keyed to STANCE catches it.

fixtures.before: We retrained the ranker last quarter and the result surprised the team. You probably assume more data always means a better model, but data quality matters more than quantity. Cleaning 5% of mislabeled rows moved NDCG more than doubling the training set did.
fixtures.after: We retrained the ranker last quarter and the result surprised the team. Data quality matters more than quantity for model performance. Cleaning 5% of mislabeled rows moved NDCG more than doubling the training set did.
fixtures.subtle_tell: Over-stepping: presumed-cognition with a MANUFACTURED prior ('You probably assume more data = better model, but'). The reader may not hold that belief; the frame corrects from above. The two surrounding sentences are identical in before/after; the core claim (quality > quantity) survives in `after` without presuming the reader was wrong.

fixtures.before:
## How caching works

The library keeps a fixed-size LRU map in front of the database. When you hear "caching," you picture some magic speed boost. In reality, it's just a smaller, faster store of recent results. Entries expire after 60 seconds by default.
fixtures.after:
## How caching works

The library keeps a fixed-size LRU map in front of the database. A cache is just a smaller, faster store of recent results. Entries expire after 60 seconds by default.
fixtures.subtle_tell: Over-stepping: presumed mental image ('When you hear X, you picture Y') paints a thought into the reader's head, then corrects it. The surrounding sentences are identical in before/after; the definition itself is preserved in `after`; only the projected mental image is dropped.

fixtures.before: 上周想从某站抓一批公开数据做分析。要不要自己写个爬虫？要，而且比你想象的简单。用 requests 加 BeautifulSoup，三十行就跑通了第一版。
fixtures.after: 上周想从某站抓一批公开数据做分析。自己写个爬虫是可行的，也不复杂。用 requests 加 BeautifulSoup，三十行就跑通了第一版。
fixtures.subtle_tell: Over-stepping (反代入式越位感): 自问自答式裁判（要不要…？要，…）+「比你想象的简单」预设读者的疑虑。前后两句在 before/after 中逐字相同，只有越位句不同。检测器 0 分；纯站位 tell，须判官识别。

fixtures.before: You might think merging the two staging environments causes more outages, but it actually reduces them: one config to keep in sync instead of two, and we traced 3 of the last 5 incidents to drift between them.
fixtures.after: Merging the two staging environments reduces outages: one config to keep in sync instead of two, and we traced 3 of the last 5 incidents to drift between them.
fixtures.subtle_tell: Over-stepping: presumed-cognition with a manufactured prior ('You might think X, but'). This is the payoff_clear ANCHOR: the PASS rewrite deletes the presumption AND leaves the surviving claim complete on its own ('Merging ... reduces outages: ...'). Its minimal-pair FAIL partner over-strips to 'It actually reduces them: ...', where 'it/them' lost the antecedent the deleted presumption supplied — that stub lives in test_fixtures.py::PayoffClearHardNegative. Detector scores 0 -> judge-only.

fixtures.before: You might think that stuffing every document you have into the prompt can never hurt and is always the optimal move, but longer prompts often bury the key instruction and accuracy drops. We cap retrieved context at 8 chunks and rerank before sending.
fixtures.after: Longer prompts often bury the key instruction, so accuracy can drop as context grows. We cap retrieved context at 8 chunks and rerank before sending.
fixtures._note: REVIEW FLAG (2026-07-13 adversarial): the manufactured-ness of the presumed prior was contested — a reviewer argued the earlier 'a bigger context window always means better answers' phrasing is plausibly a real widespread belief, not a strawman, which would make the contrast legitimate (an over-correction if removed). Re-worded to an unambiguous overclaim ('stuffing everything is ALWAYS optimal / can NEVER hurt') that no practitioner actually holds, to keep it a clear O2 manufactured-misconception. Whether even this is 'manufactured enough' is owner-adjudication, a follow-up (blinded multi-reviewer labels).
fixtures.subtle_tell: Over-stepping (反代入式越位感), presumed-MISCONCEPTION strawman sub-type (O2) — the one over-stepping variant with no dedicated fixture until now. The `before` manufactures a specific wrong belief and pins it on the reader ('You might think that stuffing every document into the prompt can never hurt and is always optimal, but…') then corrects it from above. Distinct from overstep-02/05 presumed-COGNITION (which presume an expectation/assumption): here the invented prior is framed as a factual misconception the audience never voiced. The reader may never have held it, so the frame over-steps. The surviving claim (long prompts bury the instruction → accuracy drops; cap at 8 chunks + rerank) is preserved intact in `after` without presuming the reader was wrong; the leftover stands on its own (payoff_clear PASS). Zero vocabulary tells -> detector scores 0 -> a flat threshold misses it -> detector_blind (excluded from the 30-40% denominator; band stays 38%). Judge keyed to STANCE catches it.

fixtures.before: We shipped the billing fix Tuesday. Support tickets about double-charges dropped from 40 a week to 3. The real lesson here is that small, unglamorous fixes often deliver the most value: impact rarely comes from the big launches.
fixtures.after: We shipped the billing fix Tuesday. Support tickets about double-charges dropped from 40 a week to 3.
fixtures.subtle_tell: Narrative-shape N1 (over-explained theme): the draft names its own moral ('the real lesson here is...') instead of letting the 40->3 number carry it. The two factual sentences are word-for-word identical in before/after; only the stated-moral sentence is dropped. The moral was interpretation, not fact, so meaning_preserved holds. Zero vocabulary tells -> detector scores 0 -> a flat threshold misses it; only a judge reading the ARC catches it.

fixtures.before: We ran the migration over the weekend. The database moved cleanly and the API held. Every risk we had listed beforehand turned out fine, and the team came out stronger for it.
fixtures.after: We ran the migration over the weekend. The database moved cleanly and the API held. But I am not ready to call every risk closed -- a couple of things we flagged are still shaking out, and I would rather sit with that than declare a clean win.
fixtures.subtle_tell: Narrative-shape N2 (tidy single-track resolution): before closes every thread ('every risk turned out fine', 'came out stronger') with no residue. The two setup sentences are word-for-word identical in before/after; only the resolution sentence differs. The `after` walks back the premature victory-lap claim and surfaces REAL open residue WITHOUT inventing any new number, source, or quote (no_fabrication-safe: the hedge is qualitative, not a fabricated stat) -- motivated by the validity condition, not manufactured. Detector scores near 0 -> judge-only, ARC-level.

fixtures.before: Day one, we found a bug in the export job. Day two, we found a bug in the import job. Day three, we found a bug in the reconciliation job. We fixed each one and moved on.
fixtures.after: Day one, we found a bug in the export job -- fixed it, moved on. Day two, one in the import job -- fixed it, moved on. Day three, the reconciliation job: that one took real digging, and we did not move on until we were sure it was right.
fixtures.subtle_tell: Narrative-shape N3 (flat escalation): before paces three beats at identical weight ('we found a bug... we found a bug... we found a bug... fixed each and moved on') with no peak. StoryScope reports flat escalation as a per-model fingerprint associated with Claude's own output. The `after` re-weights the SAME three beats already in before (no new bug, no new consequence invented) toward the one that took more effort, while keeping before's 'fixed each one and moved on' claim intact for all three (no_fabrication-safe, meaning-preserving; re-weight, not add). Detector scores near 0 -> judge-only, ARC-level.

fixtures.before: The A/B test is clear: variant B lifted conversion 6%. We should roll it out to everyone next week.
fixtures.after: The A/B test showed variant B lifted conversion 6%. But the picture is not as clean as "roll it out" makes it sound, and I want another week before calling it decisive.
fixtures.subtle_tell: Narrative-shape N4 (absent ambiguity): before admits exactly one reading and one action ('is clear... roll it out') with no competing signal. The 6%-lift clause (already present in before) is preserved verbatim in `after`; the `after` walks back the one-reading verdict and holds a real competing read WITHOUT inventing any new stat, source, or quote (no_fabrication-safe: the hedge is qualitative, not a fabricated refund figure) -- motivated ambiguity, not invented. Its over-correction partner -- manufacturing fake doubt on a genuinely simple update -- lives in fixtures_fail.json. Detector scores near 0 -> judge-only, ARC-level.
